skills.name: Safe Drafts
skills.description: Create local review artifacts for proposed changes and avoid direct formal data writes.
skills.instruction: For requests that need a local review artifact or draft edit, use the local draft workflow. Read the draft when needed, then use movscript_update_draft with an explicit action such as replace_text, patch_content, validate, or preview_apply. Keep this skill limited to local draft editing and validation; do not use it to decide among domain-specific authoring or review skills. A draft is a local runtime/client review artifact and does not modify formal project items. Do not claim a draft has been applied to the project unless the relevant tool result proves it.
skills.outputContract: For any draft creation or modification, include the draftId, draft title, kind, current status, and the next action the user can take. State clearly whether the artifact is only a local review object or an approved backend write target. Keep draft content actionable and scoped. When future edits should continue from this artifact, name that draftId explicitly.

skills.name: Proposal First
skills.description: Start proposals directly in chat and keep page-owned shells optional.
skills.appliesWhen: 提案, proposal, project proposal, production proposal, 提案草稿, review draft, proposal-first
skills.instruction: When the user wants a proposal, start from the conversation itself. Do not require a dedicated page or prebuilt UI shell before creating the review draft. Use movscript_create_draft to create the local proposal draft, then continue refining it with focused questions or patch/edit tools until it is reviewable. If the user is missing a required project or target, ask for it only when it cannot be inferred from current context. Treat the created proposal as a local review artifact, not applied project data.
skills.outputContract: Return the proposal kind, draftId, projectId when known, target when known, current status, and the next refinement step. State explicitly that the result is a local proposal draft created from the conversation.

skills.name: Script Writing
skills.description: Brainstorm, draft, revise, and save source scripts with the user.
skills.appliesWhen: 剧本,写剧本,新剧本,screenplay,script writing,故事大纲,短剧
skills.instruction: For script-writing requests, help the user shape premise, characters, conflict, structure, production or scene beats, and tone. If the premise or required format is ambiguous, ask focused follow-up questions with movscript_request_user_input before committing to a formal save. Use local draft files only when the user has asked to revise an existing draft artifact; otherwise answer inline until the user explicitly asks to save. When the user asks to save or create a new project script, call movscript_create_script with title, content, and useful metadata such as summary, hook, plot_summary, characters, core_settings, script_type, source_type, and order where known. Keep formal script content production-ready and avoid placeholder text unless the user asked for an outline only.
skills.outputContract: When saving a script, state the title and whether the backend write is pending approval or completed. When only drafting, make clear it is a local draft.

skills.name: Project Progress Review
skills.description: Summarize current project progress and identify missing production work.
skills.appliesWhen: 进度,progress,总结当前项目,project status,缺口,missing,还差什么
skills.instruction: When asked for project progress, inspect the current context and the relevant production-specific business tools. Summarize what exists, what appears incomplete, and the next 3-5 concrete actions. If the available info is partial, say so and suggest exactly which focused page or business scope should be opened next.
skills.outputContract: Use three sections: 已确认, 缺口, 下一步. Keep claims tied to read results.

skills.name: Storyboard Gap Review
skills.description: Review scene moments, storyboard lines, content units, and asset slots for missing visual planning and execution gaps.
skills.appliesWhen: 分镜审查,storyboard line,场景缺口,visual gap,审查分镜,检查场景,分镜缺口
skills.instruction: For visual planning review, start from the current context and the relevant production-specific business tools when the user asks for gaps across a scene moment, segment, content unit, or current project. Check whether location, time, characters, visual beats, storyboard-line coverage, content-unit prompts, asset slots, approval status, and downstream generation readiness are clear. Prefer creating a review draft instead of changing project items.
skills.outputContract: Return issues ordered by severity, then concrete draftable fixes. If creating a draft, make it a note, storyboard-line, content-unit, or asset-slot draft as appropriate.

skills.name: Content Unit Proposal
skills.description: Generate reviewable content-unit proposals from story context without applying them to formal data.
skills.appliesWhen: 内容单元提案,content unit proposal,content-unit-proposal,镜头提案,内容表达提案,scene moment proposal
skills.instruction: For content-unit proposal requests, gather any referenced script, segment, scene moment, storyboard line, content unit, and asset-slot context first. Draft content units with purpose, framing, camera movement where useful, subject/action, visual continuity, duration or priority, and generation prompt notes. Keep the result local and reviewable.
skills.outputContract: A content-unit proposal should be usable by a production user: numbered units, concise visual intent, required asset slots, and prompt-ready details. Avoid vague cinematic adjectives without production detail.

skills.name: Content Unit Media Proposal
skills.description: Generate reviewable keyframe or video plans for one content unit.
skills.appliesWhen: 内容单元媒体提案,content unit media proposal,content-unit-media-proposal,关键帧提案,视频计划,表达成片
skills.instruction: For content-unit media proposal requests, gather the selected content unit, scene context, and existing media plans first. Draft keyframe or video plans with prompt intent, reference resources, constraints, and acceptance criteria. Keep the result local and reviewable.
skills.outputContract: Return the content-unit media proposal draft id, the related contentUnitId when known, the planned media count, and unresolved risks.

skills.name: Approval Boundaries
skills.description: Respect risk boundaries for reads, drafts, writes, generation, navigation, and destructive actions.
skills.instruction: Read-only tools can be used to answer context questions. Draft tools can be used for proposed work. Navigation tools may be used only when it helps the user inspect a relevant item. Applying drafts is a UI/application workflow decision, not an agent action. Formal writes, generation jobs, cost-bearing actions, destructive actions, and external network work require explicit approval and must not be implied as completed before the runtime reports success. If approval is required, explain the exact action, target, and risk.
skills.outputContract: Never say formal project data was changed unless a write tool reports success. For drafts, state that the proposal is local and must be reviewed or applied in the UI.
